diagrams.title: B+ Tree Structure
diagrams.description: A complete B+ tree showing root, intermediate nodes, and leaf nodes with linked list
elements: Root node with keys for routing
Intermediate nodes (internal nodes) containing only keys
Leaf nodes containing actual key-value pairs and data pointers
Horizontal linked list connecting leaf nodes
All leaf nodes at same level (balanced)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding B+ tree properties for efficient indexing and range queries

diagrams.title: Multi-Level Index Architecture
diagrams.description: Three-level index structure showing how disk accesses are minimized
elements: Level 1: Sparse index on data file (one entry per block)
Level 2: Sparse index on Level 1 index
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how multi-level indexing reduces disk I/O operations

diagrams.title: Clustered vs Non-Clustered Index
diagrams.description: Comparison showing physical arrangement differences between clustered and non-clustered indexes
elements: Clustered: Data physically sorted by index key, only one per table
Non-Clustered: Separate index structure, multiple per table, leaf points to data row
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when to use each index type for optimal performance

diagrams.title: Hash Index with Collision
diagrams.description: Hash index showing bucket structure and collision handling via chaining
elements: Hash function H(key) = key mod n
Collision: multiple keys mapping to same bucket
Chaining: linked list of records in overflow area
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hash index implementation and collision resolution
